experience.title: Sales Manager
experience.description: Responsible for driving and leading the business sales strategy and direction of corporate programmes tailored towards blue chip and tier 1 companies for the hotel i.e. participating and hosting events that will promote the awareness of the company and its credentials. • Leading the daily activities of MICE sales with a focus on building long term customer relationships that lead to the achievement of sales objectives and repeat business. • Attend weekly Sales Strategy and Sales Meetings as well as providing periodic forecasts and budgets as required. • Respond to sales enquires and arrange site inspection trips to hotel by corporate clients maintaining good relations with the VIP and key clients of the Company. • Proactively sell the venue to new clients in building and strengthening relationships enabling future bookings for the hotel. • Proactive account management of existing clients to grow revenue. • Supervise event planning and delivery with overall responsibility. • Work closely with and collaborate with all departments and operational staff to ensure consistently high standards. • Implement and execute all sales objectives and action plans to reach and exceed targets set. • Solicit and serve transient and group business and meetings. • Maximise up selling opportunities by proposing additional events and activities suitable and complimenting to the MICE bookings. • Management of client contracts and overseeing the Invoicing process to ensure payments are correctly processed. • Obtain client feedback after each event verbally and through ‘Thank you’ letter and Guest questionnaire. • Monitor and regularly review competitors to ensure that the hotel remains competitive for accommodation as well as a conference and events venue. • Identifying business trends and marketing opportunities which can improve the hotel’s performance. • Supporting the hotels service and relationship strategy, driving customer loyalty by delivering service of excellence.

experience.title: Hotel House Manager, Grange Bloomsbury Town House Collection
experience.description:
Company: 51-200 employees • Founded 1985 • Privately Held • Hospitality
Outline: A collection of 4-star boutique Hotels, housing 254 bedrooms near Bloomsbury Oxford Street. Key Responsibilities: • Directed and steered the Hotel Reception, controlled the allocation of guest bedrooms, met and greeted clients, dealt with all guest complaints and comments, conducted pre-shift head of department meetings, ensured all department heads were aware of bedroom availability, applicable bedroom rates for the day, in-house functions, VIP arrivals, group arrivals and planned site-visits. • Planned work schedules, trained and motivated staff, ensured the team delivered an effective and enjoyable experience to all guests, assisted in organising site visits, ensured show rooms/conferences and events areas were prepared, conducted employee reviews and appraisals, managed employee roster, attendance and payroll, assisted Company HR Management in identifying and developing talent, managed all aspects of Health & Safety, Security and Fire Safety. Key Achievements: • Supported the hotel’s business development objectives, through collaborating the Front of House Manager, Reservations Manager, Conference & Events Manager and Food & Beverage Manager to identify business opportunities, which resulted in developing a continuous pipeline of reactive leads through networking events. • Elected to deputise as General Manager in the absence of superior, which involved managing the team and all operations within the hotel. • Successfully managed the opening of the Grange St. Paul’s Hotel and Grange Tower Bridge Hotel, which included room inventory and website content.

experience.title: Assistant Revenue Manager, Grange Hotels Head Office
experience.description:
Company: 51-200 employees • Founded 1985 • Privately Held • Hospitality
Outline: 16 London properties, and 1 in Bracknell, including 2091 bedrooms in total. Key Achievements: • Oversaw revenue management and distribution strategy of each hotel within the group and managed day-to-day operations, analysed overall monthly hotel performance and provided summary report with recommendations to improve long term strategies, conducted quarterly property performance review and developed strategic and tactical actions, prepared outline for and supported the annual revenue budget process. • Implemented training and development initiatives for 15 staff members, through managing teams’ performance, holding 1-2-1s, as well as maintaining individual development plans, supporting the organisation’s strategic objectives in the personal and professional development of key staff members, whilst strengthening the organisation’s team. • Supported the hotel’s cost control measures, by reducing the cost of distribution, through identifying new less expensive means of delivering business. • Developed positive and profitable relationships with clients, by building strong customer loyalty through services and assistance, driving customer retention and satisfaction levels. • Exceeded YoY RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) including 2017 by 1.34%, 2016 by 0.45%, 2015 by 3.13%, 2014 by 2.21%, 2013 by 2.42% and 2012 by 8.98% during the London Olympics. • Played a pivotal role in expanding the organisation’s promotional campaigns, in collaboration with Company Managing Directors, as well as Front of House, Sales, Marketing and IT Teams, by setting up the hotel’s website, search engine optimisation, social media and other marketing and PR activities.
